The Server class¶

class services.server.Server(object)¶

The Server class is the main public class to be instanciated and use directly by the user.

Public methods¶

services.server.Server(response=ImmediateAndNonBlocking())
Parameters:response (a subclass of Services.Response.Response.) – an instance of a class which can handle a response for POST request

When creating an instance, a response should be specified by the user. See Services.Responses for more information. By default, the server will not block and handle services in a different thread immediately. The server installs a handler to serve static pages at the location specified by the environment variable SEMPERPY_WEB, so any file from that location down can be served as static pages (see Services.HTML.HTML for more information).

services.server.run(port=8080)¶
Parameters:port (int) – the port number on which the server should listen for connections

Starts the server on the specified port.

classmethod services.server.registerService(service, callable)¶
Parameters:
  • service (list) – name of the service, or list describing a hierarchy of names of services
  • callable (callable) – a function, an callable object or a bound method, to be called when the specified service in invoked.

Registers a service using the name of hierarchy of names on the POST http request. What the callable receives depends on what the client sends. The client is expected to send a dictionary d, the service callable receives **d. See the Calculator example for more information. The callable should then look like:

def callable(**kargs):
        return <arbitrary python structure>

The python structure returned by the callable is encoded into json and sent back to the client. If anything goes wrong during the processing of a request, a Services.Errors.ServiceError or any subclass should be raised.

classmethod services.server.registerPage(page, callable)¶
Parameters:
  • page (list) – name of the page, or list describing a hierarchy of names of pages
  • callable (callable) – a function, an callable object or a bound method, to be called when the specified page in invoked.

Registers a page using the name of hierarchy of names on the GET http request. The callable receives a twisted request as the sole parameter:

def callable(request):
        return string containing HTML

Private methods¶

services.server.render_GET(request)¶

implements the twisted required method to answer a GET request. By default, it displays the hierarchy of registered services and pages on this server.

services.server.nested(request, all, prefix)¶

Recursively generated the list of services and pages for display purposes, not documented.

classmethod services.server.register(where, service, object)¶

Called by registerService and registerPage to actually make the registration.
